column24: Social media has fundamentally transformed human communication by making it instant, borderless, and constant. People no longer need to be physically present or even in the same time zone to maintain relationships — a quick message, comment, or reaction is enough to stay connected. However, this convenience has come at a cost. Conversations have become shorter and more surface-level, with emojis and memes often replacing deeper discussions.
column25: Yes — I've observed situations where online arguments between friends or acquaintances on platforms like Instagram or WhatsApp spilled over into real life, causing lasting damage to those relationships. In one instance, a heated exchange in a group chat over a social issue led to two close friends completely cutting ties, something that likely wouldn't have escalated as quickly in a face-to-face conversation.
